task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST application window is open with '4StoreyBuilding' visible in the title bar or project component tree.

task_completion.description: Create a Day Schedule named 'SiteLt-Sch' for dusk-to-dawn operation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A schedule named 'SiteLt-Sch' is visible in the component tree or a properties dialog. The hourly profile should show ON (e.g., 1.0 or 100%) for hours 1-6 and 19-24, and OFF (0.0) for hours 7-18.

task_completion.description: Establish an Electric Meter named 'Ext Lighting Meter'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A meter named 'Ext Lighting Meter' is visible in the component tree (typically under Utility & Economics or Meters) or in a properties dialog.

task_completion.description: Create an Exterior Lighting load named 'Parking & Facade Lts' with a 4.5 kW rating, assigned to the new schedule and meter
task_completion.visual_evidence: An exterior lighting component named 'Parking & Facade Lts' is visible. Its properties dialog shows a power/capacity of 4.5 kW, the schedule set to 'SiteLt-Sch', and the meter set to 'Ext Lighting Meter'.

task_completion.description: Run the annual simulation and save the project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST simulation progress window (showing DOE-2.2 calculation phases) is visible, or a 'Simulation Complete' message is shown, followed by evidence of saving the project (e.g., File -> Save menu highlighted or save dialog).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the eQUEST GUI rather than directly editing the .inp file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the eQUEST graphical interface being used to create components, not Notepad or another text editor modifying the 4StoreyBuilding.inp file.

integrity.description: The simulation was actually run within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard eQUEST/DOE-2 calculation progress window or results viewer should be visible, not just a terminal command or mocked output.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by running automated setup scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: No PowerShell or Python scripts are seen being executed in a terminal to automatically inject the components into the project file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Software: eQUEST 3.65 build 7175. Project: 4StoreyBuilding. Target Schedule: 'SiteLt-Sch' with dusk-to-dawn profile (ON/1.0 for hours 1-6 and 19-24; OFF/0.0 for hours 7-18). Target Meter: 'Ext Lighting Meter'. Target Load: 'Parking & Facade Lts' with 4.5 kW power. The load must be assigned to both the created schedule and meter. Note: Setup/export scripts are missing from the directory, so the agent must complete the task manually via the eQUEST GUI.
